behaviors.description: Extract all direct and transitive dependencies from manifest
behaviors.justification: Requires parsing real manifest files and resolving transitive dependencies through filesystem I/O and likely subprocess calls (npm ls, pip freeze, maven dependency:tree). Mocking the resolver hides parsing bugs, edge cases in format handling (JSON, YAML, TOML variants), and transitive resolution failures.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real manifest file with both direct and transitive dependencies, run the extraction, verify all discovered including deep transitive chain

behaviors.description: Vulnerability database query matches exact version ranges in manifest
behaviors.justification: Requires real HTTP calls to external vulnerability database API. A mocked API could pass with matching response format but diverge on real API behavior: schema changes, new fields, missing data for obscure packages, or incorrect version-range matching logic that the real API would expose.
behaviors.test_description: Query a real vulnerability API (or sandbox) with test packages at specific versions, verify returned CVEs correctly match the declared version ranges

behaviors.description: Sort vulnerabilities by severity then by CVSS score within severity
behaviors.justification: Pure sorting algorithm with no I/O or external dependencies. Test data can fully cover severity tiers (Critical/High/Medium/Low) and CVSS score tie-breaking without needing real infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Create test CVE results with mixed severities and CVSS scores, verify output is sorted by severity descending then CVSS descending within each severity

behaviors.description: Modify manifest file to bump only affected dependency version
behaviors.justification: Requires real filesystem I/O and format-specific manifest manipulation. Different formats (JSON, requirements.txt, Gemfile, etc.) have format-preservation edge cases: comments, whitespace, quoting, YAML anchors. A mock wouldn't catch string-manipulation bugs or format corruption.
behaviors.test_description: Write a real manifest with multiple dependencies and comments, bump one version, read back and verify only that version changed and original formatting is preserved

behaviors.description: Create pull request on real repository with single-dependency diff
behaviors.justification: Requires real subprocess (git commit, git push) and real external API (GitHub/GitLab PR creation). Mocking git or the PR API would miss real failures in git diff generation, branch creation, remote push, or API payload formatting.
behaviors.test_description: Set up a real git repository, apply the dependency version change, push to a real repository (or sandbox environment), create PR via API and verify the diff contains only the single dependency line change

behaviors.description: Identify major version bump as breaking change using semver
behaviors.justification: Pure semantic versioning comparison logic with no I/O. Test data can exhaustively cover semver cases: major bumps (1.0.0→2.0.0), minor (1.0.0→1.1.0), patch (1.0.0→1.0.1), pre-release versions, and edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Test semver comparison with various version transitions, verify only major version increments are flagged as breaking changes

behaviors.description: Skip PR creation when breaking change detected, surface review flag
behaviors.justification: Must verify control flow: when a breaking change is detected, the PR creation API must not be invoked AND the review flag must be set in output. Integration level allows mocking the PR API to verify non-invocation while testing the conditional logic, catching bugs where PR is accidentally created despite breaking change detection.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger a breaking change scenario (major version bump), verify output contains review flag set to true and the PR creation API is mocked and never called
